title: Grafana Organization and Folder Permission Change Audit
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:audit` RBAC changes on folders, teams, org roles
app: Grafana audit logging enabled for permission actions
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit"
| search (folder OR organization OR team OR "permission") AND (action="*update*" OR action="*add*" OR action="*remove*" OR action="*delete*")
| stats count, values(permission) as perms, latest(_time) as t by object_uid, user, action
| where count >= 1
| sort -t
description: Overly broad folder grants expose customer data on shared Grafana stacks. Auditing permission mutations provides detective control and speeds reviews when suspicious sharing is reported.
value: Supports least-privilege programs and demonstrates access monitoring to auditors.
implementation: Enrich `object_uid` with folder title via lookup. Route alerts on `action=remove` paired with `role=Editor` in public folders. Integrate with ITSM change tickets for expected bulk updates.
visualization: Table (user × folder × action), timeline, count by role granted.
grandmaExplanation: We record when someone changes who can see which folders of charts. That creates a paper trail if sensitive data was shared too widely.
